falsification_conditions.condition: The price of the asset rises above the 200-day moving average by 10% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would indicate a strong bullish trend contradicting the 'HOLD' signal and suggesting the pattern is not a bottom but a continuation of an uptrend.

falsification_conditions.condition: Trading volume exceeds 200% of the 20-day average volume on at least three consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Such a surge in volume would signal a blowoff top or extreme market participation, invalidating the 'UNKNOWN' verdict by confirming a clear directional move.

falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 Index closes above its all-time high by 5% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A major index breaking its all-time high would falsify the pattern's similarity to a historical bottom, proving the current call incorrect by demonstrating a bullish breakout.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
